famous quote:
"Pride gets no pleasure out of having something, only out of having more of it than the next man... It is the comparison that makes you proud: the pleasure of being above the rest. Once the element of competition is gone, pride is gone."
-- C.S. Lewis (Mere Christianity)
Interview Text:
Debra:

(part 2)

She told a story about how part of one of her visions was her digging something beautiful out of the ground and she described how when she had that vision her hands just started scratching into the carpet, as if she were digging in dirt. She described several other visions to me and the important themes each emphasized. 

She said one of the themes is "The cross. The cross. The cross. It always comes back to the cross. But one thing the church doesn't spend nearly enough time on is the grave. Ross, I don't like this because it sounds like I am complaining that the church focuses on the cross instead of the grave. Actually, this is 2 seperate subjects. When I said, 'The cross! The cross! It always comes back to the cross!' I meant that hundreds of times in my life I have had visions of the cross when I am hurting or emotionally wounded in some way. Isaiah 53 says Surely you have taken up this infirmity... I have had visions of the crown of thorns, the flogging, etc. By his wounds, I am healed. The cross has been a HUGE part of my healing journey." 

For example, she told me that while praying one time she suddenly saw an image of Jesus hanging on the cross and he took her fears and false guilt and emotional brokenness from her, opened his chest up, and put them inside until all of them were gone.
Music Video Instructions: Does this describe our constant pursuit of God?
